title: Homo Deus: A Brief History of Tomorrow
author: Yuval Noah Harari
themes: The new human agenda: immortality, bliss, and divinity
The end of famine, plague, and war as historical forces
The rise of dataism as the successor to humanism
The emergence of a 'useless class' rendered obsolete by AI and automation
The decoupling of intelligence from consciousness
The three great threats: technological disruption, ecological collapse, and the rise of AI
Algorithms as the new authority — knowing me better than I know myself
The merger of biotechnology and information technology to 'upgrade' Homo sapiens
Religion, nationalism, and humanism as stories that shaped the modern world
awards: Goodreads Choice Award Finalist — Science & Technology (2017)
authorBiography: Yuval Noah Harari (born February 24, 1976, in Kiryat Ata, Israel) is an Israeli historian, philosopher, and bestselling author. He is a professor in the Department of History at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, where he specializes in world history, medieval history, and military history. He earned his PhD from the University of Oxford in 2002 and has published widely in both academic and popular forums. Harari first gained global recognition with Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind (2011/2014), followed by Homo Deus: A Brief History of Tomorrow (2015/2016) and 21 Lessons for the 21st Century (2018). Together, the trio has sold tens of millions of copies and been translated into more than 65 languages. He is known for blending macro-history, evolutionary biology, economics, and philosophy of mind to challenge assumptions about free will, consciousness, and humanity's future. Harari has advised global leaders, including at the World Economic Forum in Davos, on technology, climate, and the long-term future of the species.
